channel.title: Comments for page "A Kind of Redemption"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Kind of Redemption"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My review from not so long ago:

This tale is about SCP-4031, and I understand just about as much as I understand the skip it originates from. It is good writing, just confusing writing. I don't know if I have too much to say on this one. I think it's a pretty good gift for someone who understands 4031 (as I imagine the creator would, heheh).

I don't think much has changed. I am going to put this at a novote just from sheer confusion — to get an upvote from me, I have to feel like I'm following something, and even reading the original skip doesn't give me much more information to go off of. But I can tell that this was a wonderful gift for Flops, and that you used the spoilered version of what SCP-4031 is to guide your writing. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale manages to somehow stir up a lot of weird, alien feelings in everyday monotony. I'm reminded of one of those "fever dream" sensations I used to have as a kid — the kinds you feel when you have the flu and wake up in the wee hours of the night, hot and sweating. You stare up at the darkness, temples continuously flaring up with a pressure that won't go away, and as your eyes adjust, fixated on the ceiling, it feels like the whole room is a thousand times larger than it ought to be. Your head is aching, and something shimmering, faraway, spins over and over on a giant track. This tale seems to remind me of being in that same kind of headspace, with the way things are described and framed.

There was hardly much depth in SCP-4031 — but here? You took the source material, fleshed out a unique setting, crafted this really quaint story around it, and ended everything on a really visceral image. And I admire it!

Tufto, thank you so much for this awesome holiday gift!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My present for Lt Flops in the Christmas Art Exchange 2019! Based on her excellent SCP-4031.

Many thanks to Nico for critting.
